page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-32 is an x86_64 architecture server instance featuring 32 dedicated virtual CPUs and 252.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides approximately 7.88 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and it is provisioned with zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent processing capabilities without resource contention from other tenants. With a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of nearly 1:8, this server type offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring substantial memory capacity relative to compute resources, avoiding the need to over-provision vCPUs simply to acquire sufficient RAM.

Given the hardware profile, the c4d-highmem-32 is designed for memory-intensive workloads. Suitable applications include in-memory databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise applications, data analytics, and memory-heavy backend services that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highmem-32 is an x86_64 architecture server configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 252.0 GB of RAM, yielding 7.88 GB of memory per core. This instance features dedicated CPU allocation to ensure predictable performance, though it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes this server type a resource-efficient option for workloads that demand significant memory capacity without requiring a proportional increase in compute cores. It is designed for memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases, caching layers, data analytics, and enterprise software suites.
